sections.title: New Vocabulary
points: FARM, ROUTINE, and NOTICE are useful words for village life.
PUNCTUATION helps make sentences clear and correct.
Daily chores can be described with verbs like MILK, COOK, and CLEAN.

sections.title: Fixing Sentences
points: A sentence needs the right punctuation mark to make sense.
Question marks, periods, and commas are the most common.
Example: 'What is the time?' uses a question mark.

sections.text: Tip: A sentence with a question needs a question mark, and a complete statement usually ends with a period.

cards.fact: A regular set of tasks done every day.
cards.mnemonic: Routine is what we do day after day.

cards.fact: A written message that gives information or instructions.
cards.mnemonic: A notice tells people what to do.

cards.fact: Marks that organize writing and show pauses or stops.
